q: Which body is responsible for providing basic amenities like street lights and water in a village?
sol: The Gram Panchayat handles local infrastructure and basic services.

q: The Gram Panchayat is accountable to which of the following?
sol: The Gram Sabha monitors the Panchayat and has the power to question its decisions.

q: Who is the administrative head of a Block (Panchayat Samiti)?
sol: The BDO coordinates the development work at the block level.

q: Which of these is a major source of income for rural local bodies?
o: Local taxes on houses and fairs
sol: Panchayats collect local taxes to fund their activities.

q: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is related to:
sol: This amendment gave constitutional status to the Panchayati Raj system.

q: What is the primary role of the Nyaya Panchayat?
sol: It provides a simple and fast way for villagers to settle small legal issues.

q: Who among the following is a non-elected government employee in the Gram Panchayat?
sol: The Secretary is appointed by the government to maintain records.

q: How many levels are there in the rural local government structure in India?
sol: It is a three-tier system: Village, Block, and District.

q: The Zila Parishad works at the ______ level.
sol: Zila Parishad is the highest tier of the Panchayati Raj.

q: What is the main purpose of reserving seats for women in Panchayats?
o: To reduce the number of men
To ensure their participation in decision-making
sol: Reservation empowers women to have a voice in local governance.

q: Who prepares the development plans for the entire district?
sol: Zila Parishad coordinates the plans of all blocks in a district.

q: Which committee recommended the three-tier Panchayati Raj system?
sol: The 1957 committee suggested this structure to ensure democracy at the bottom.

q: A Gram Panchayat is divided into smaller areas called:
sol: Each ward elects one representative to the Panchayat.

q: Who presides over the meetings of the Gram Sabha?
sol: The Sarpanch (Panchayat President) leads the meetings.

q: Which body implements the 'Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana' at the village level?
sol: Local bodies help identify beneficiaries and implement housing schemes.

q: What is the minimum age to contest a Panchayat election?
sol: A person must be at least 21 years old to stand as a candidate.

q: The term of a Gram Panchayat is for how many years?
sol: Elections are held every five years.

q: Which of these is a function of the Zila Parishad?
o: Advising the State Government on district development
sol: It acts as a link between the local bodies and the state government.

q: Who is the executive officer of the Zila Parishad?
sol: The CEO, usually an IAS officer, manages the Zila Parishad's administration.

q: The Panchayat Samiti is also known as:
sol: Names vary across states but 'Janpad' or 'Block Samiti' are common.

q: Who provides the funds to the Gram Panchayat for large projects?
sol: Most development funds come through government schemes and grants.

q: Maintenance of land records in rural areas is primarily done by the:
sol: The Patwari is responsible for measuring land and keeping records.

q: Which of these is NOT a tier of the Panchayati Raj?
sol: Municipal Corporations are part of urban (city) local government.

q: What happens if a Gram Panchayat is dissolved before its term ends?
o: The village remains without a government
Fresh elections must be held within 6 months
sol: Elections must be held to ensure democratic continuity.

q: The Gram Sabha consists of:
o: Only the rich people of the village
All adults (18+) registered in the voter list
sol: Every adult villager is a member of the Gram Sabha.

q: Social auditing by the Gram Sabha helps in:
o: Transparency and checking the use of funds
sol: It allows people to check if the work was actually done correctly.

q: The head of the Zila Parishad is known as the:
sol: The Chairperson is elected from among the members of the Zila Parishad.

q: How does local government strengthen democracy?
o: By giving all power to one person
By encouraging people's participation in governance
sol: It allows people to manage their own affairs at the grassroots level.

q: The Gram Panchayat Secretary is appointed by:
sol: It is a government post to ensure administrative continuity.

q: Which level coordinates the work of all the Block Samitis in a district?
sol: Zila Parishad functions as the apex body for the district.

q: A dispute over a small property in a village can be settled by the:
sol: Local disputes are the jurisdiction of the Nyaya Panchayat.

q: The 'Panchs' are elected for a term of:
sol: Like the Sarpanch, Ward Members serve for five years.

q: Which of these is a responsibility of the Panchayat Samiti?
o: Supervising the work of several Gram Panchayats
sol: It oversees the development blocks and coordinates between village and district.

q: What is the role of the Gram Sabha in selecting beneficiaries?
o: It discusses and approves the list of BPL families
sol: The Gram Sabha ensures the right people get government benefits.

q: The word 'Panch' literally means:
sol: Traditionally, it refers to the assembly of five elders.

q: In which state did the Panchayati Raj system first start in 1959?
sol: Nagaur district in Rajasthan was the first to adopt it.

q: Who prepares the budget for the Zila Parishad?
sol: The administrative staff prepares it for approval by the members.

q: Rural local government is important because:
o: It is easier to control people
Local people understand their problems better
sol: Proximity to the issues leads to better solutions.

q: The Vice-Sarpanch is elected by:
o: The Ward Members (Panchs) from among themselves
sol: Panchs choose the Vice-Sarpanch to assist the Sarpanch.

q: What is the primary function of the 'Patwari' regarding the Panchayat?
sol: Patwaris manage the land administration essential for rural planning.

q: Who oversees the implementation of health schemes in a village?
sol: Local health and sanitation are key Panchayat functions.

q: Which of the following describes 'Decentralization of Power'?
o: Giving all power to the King
Distributing power from the center to local levels
Taking away the right to vote
sol: Panchayati Raj is the best example of decentralization in India.

q: Public property in a village includes:
o: Village wells, grazing grounds, and roads
sol: Anything maintained by the Panchayat for common use is public property.

q: If a villager is unhappy with a road project, they can raise it in the:
sol: The Gram Sabha is the platform for public grievances.

q: Who monitors the functioning of the Block Development Officer?
o: The District Magistrate and Zila Parishad
sol: The higher administrative levels supervise the block level.

q: Which of these is a mandatory function of the Gram Panchayat?
sol: Drinking water is a basic necessity the Panchayat must provide.

q: Reservation for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in Panchayats is based on:
sol: This ensures fair representation for marginalized communities.

q: What is a 'Vikas Patra'?
o: A development report/card for the village
sol: It tracks the progress of various development schemes.

q: The Panchayat Samiti generally consists of:
o: All Sarpanchs of the block and elected members
sol: It is composed of elected representatives and village heads.

q: Why is the Gram Panchayat Secretary important?
o: To record minutes of meetings and keep accounts
sol: The Secretary ensures official records are correct and legal.

q: Who has the power to verify the work done by the Gram Panchayat?
o: The Gram Sabha and higher government officials
sol: Regular audits and Sabha meetings verify the work.

q: Which body ensures that funds are distributed among all Gram Panchayats in a district?
sol: Zila Parishad manages the financial allocations for the district.

q: The concept of 'Gram Swaraj' was emphasized by:
sol: Gandhi believed that villages should be self-governing units.

q: What is the function of the District Planning Committee?
o: Consolidating plans of Panchayats and Municipalities
sol: It integrates rural and urban plans for the whole district.

q: An adult in India who has the right to vote is anyone who is:
sol: Universal Adult Franchise grants voting rights at 18.

q: The administrative work of the village includes maintaining records of:
sol: Vital statistics are crucial for planning and legal purposes.

q: Who is the political head of the District level local government?
sol: The elected Chairperson leads the Zila Parishad.

q: Can the Gram Sabha reject a project proposed by the Panchayat?
o: Yes, if they find it unnecessary or wasteful
Only if the BDO says so
Only if the Sarpanch allows it
sol: The Sabha has the ultimate power to approve or reject plans.

q: Which of these is a way for a Panchayat to earn revenue?
o: Charging for stalls in village fairs
Selling the village land to other countries
sol: Fairs and markets are traditional sources of local income.

q: Rural governance allows for 'Direct Democracy' through which body?
sol: Because all members meet and decide directly, it is direct democracy.

q: What is the duty of the BDO regarding the Panchayat Samiti?
o: To carry out the decisions made by the Samiti
sol: The BDO is the executive arm of the block level body.

q: Who is responsible for the 'Mid-day Meal' monitoring in a village school?
o: Gram Panchayat members and school committees
sol: Local government monitors social welfare schemes.

q: The Gram Sabha must meet at least ______ times a year.
o: Twice (or more depending on the state)
sol: Regular meetings are essential for keeping the Panchayat in check.

q: Which of these issues is most likely to be discussed in a Gram Sabha?
o: A plan to repair the village well
New laws for the Supreme Court
sol: Local development issues are the core of Sabha discussions.

q: What is 'Self-Reliance' in a village context?
o: The ability to meet basic needs using local resources
Having no connection with the outside world
sol: It promotes sustainable and independent growth.

q: The 'Election Commission' of the ______ conducts Panchayat elections.
sol: The State Election Commission handles local body polls.

q: Why are committees formed in a Gram Panchayat?
o: To focus on specific tasks like education or construction
To increase the number of members
sol: Committees allow for specialized attention to different sectors.

q: Transparency in governance means:
o: Making information accessible to the public
Allowing only rich people to see files
sol: Transparency ensures honesty and prevents corruption.

q: Which level of government provides a link between the State and the Village?
sol: The intermediate and district tiers bridge the gap.

q: How does a Nyaya Panchayat punish a guilty person?
o: Sending them to jail for years
sol: They deal with minor cases and can only impose small fines.

q: Who is invited to attend Gram Sabha meetings?
o: All registered voters of the village
sol: Every registered voter is invited and expected to participate.

q: The 'Panchayat Samiti' works for a ______ area.
sol: It covers multiple Gram Panchayats within a block.

q: What is the main advantage of local government for the people?
o: They don't have to pay any taxes
They have a direct say in how their money is spent
They don't have to follow any laws
sol: Direct participation ensures funds are used as per local needs.

q: Which official maintains the 'Register of Births and Deaths'?
sol: It is a key administrative duty of the Secretary.

q: In a democracy, where does the ultimate power lie?
sol: Citizens hold the power through voting and participation.

q: What can a villager do if they find corruption in the Panchayat?
o: Report it to the Gram Sabha or higher authorities
sol: Legal and democratic channels exist for reporting corruption.

q: The reservation of seats for SC, ST, and Women is:
sol: The 73rd Amendment makes these reservations compulsory.

q: Who is responsible for street cleaning in a rural area?
sol: Sanitation is a core responsibility of the local Panchayat.

q: The village assembly that oversees the Panchayat is the:
sol: The Gram Sabha is the 'watchdog' of the Panchayat.

q: What is the importance of 'Grassroots' in democracy?
o: It refers to the grass in the park
It means starting democracy from the basic level of people
It means the top level of government
It means moving to the forest
sol: Grassroots means involving ordinary people at the local level.

q: The Chairperson of the Panchayat Samiti is elected by:
o: Indirectly by the members of the Samiti
sol: Members of the Samiti elect their head from among themselves.

q: Which of these can be a project of a Gram Panchayat?
sol: Health centers are essential local development projects.

q: Who is the head of the district's overall administration?
sol: The DM manages the administration and maintains law and order.

q: What is the primary language of communication in a village Panchayat?
sol: Local language ensures everyone understands and participates.

q: Does the Zila Parishad have its own sources of tax?
o: Yes, it can levy certain taxes and fees
sol: Zila Parishads can collect certain fees and taxes as per state laws.

q: Who can vote in the election of a Ward Member (Panch)?
o: Only the residents of that specific ward
sol: Wards are specific electoral divisions of a village.

q: The goal of local government is to provide 'Good Governance' which means:
o: Efficient, transparent, and responsive service to people
sol: Good governance aims at the welfare of all citizens.

q: What happens to the money collected through taxes by the Panchayat?
o: It is given to the Sarpanch as a gift
It is used for village development and maintenance
It is sent to the World Bank
sol: Local funds are meant for local public services.

q: The meetings of the Panchayat Samiti are attended by:
o: Sarpanchs and elected members of that block
sol: It is a representative body for the block's villages.

q: Which of these is a challenge for rural local bodies?
o: Lack of awareness and funds in some areas
Everyone wanting to be a member
sol: Limited resources and low literacy can be hurdles.

q: The Gram Panchayat building is often used for:
o: Keeping personal items of the Panch
sol: It is a public office for village business.

q: Who has the power to remove a corrupt Sarpanch?
o: Higher authorities or through a vote in the Gram Sabha/Panchayat
Only the villagers through a protest
sol: Legal procedures exist for the removal of elected officials.

q: The local government in rural areas is part of which system?
sol: Panchayati Raj is the name for rural local government in India.

q: Which body ensures that schools in the village are working properly?
o: Gram Panchayat and its education committee
sol: Panchayats have committees for education and health.

q: What is the primary purpose of the three-tier system?
o: To make it harder to rule
To ensure effective governance from village to district
To increase the number of politicians
sol: The levels allow for better planning and implementation.

q: In a Gram Sabha, can every member give their opinion?
o: Yes, it is a platform for every adult member to participate
Only if they have a college degree
sol: The strength of a Sabha lies in open discussion.

q: Who handles the electricity supply in a village?
o: State Electricity Boards with help from the Panchayat
sol: Local bodies coordinate with state departments for utilities.

q: Which of these best describes a 'Voter List'?
o: A list of all children in a school
A list of people eligible to vote in an election
A list of all products in a shop
sol: The electoral roll ensures only eligible people vote.

q: Is the participation of people necessary for the success of Panchayati Raj?
o: No, the government can do everything
Yes, it is the soul of grassroots democracy
Only if the people are rich
sol: Democracy fails if people do not take interest.

q: What is the ultimate aim of the local government?
o: To improve the quality of life in rural areas
sol: Welfare and development are the primary goals.
